note: Curated from community BSOD threads, vendor advisories, and storage forums. 'level' applies to Native NVMe (nvmedisk.sys) operation. Patterns: controller substring (case-insensitive), firmware exact (case-insensitive) or '*' wildcard. Prefix with '=' for exact controller match. Unknown entries are treated as 'proceed with caution' in the UI.

entries.note: Pre-firmware-update build. Update to 4B2QJXD7 via Samsung Magician before enabling Native NVMe.

Community reports isolate 990 Pro 2TB random-write degradation after 7B2QJXD7. Benchmark before/after and update firmware if Samsung publishes a replacement. NEEDS VALIDATION: separate community sources instead name 4B2QJXD7 as a disappearing-drive/BSOD revision fixed by 6B2QJXD7 — the exact bad/fixed revisions for the native-stack case are unconfirmed against a first-party Samsung advisory.
Reported stable on 4B2QJXD7 and later except separately flagged firmware builds.
Known stutter with older firmware. Update to 5B2QGXA8+ before enabling Native NVMe.
Reported stable on 5B2QGXA8 and later.
Community reports tie SN850X to Critical Failure / boot-loop behavior under nvmedisk.sys. Keep this model on stornvme.sys unless you have local recovery coverage and a known-good firmware result.
WD 2TB HMB BSOD fix firmware. Firmware 731130WD or later is the vendor-recommended baseline before evaluating nvmedisk.sys.
2TB SN770 HMB BSOD risk before firmware 731130WD. Update firmware first; nvmedisk.sys can compound instability while the HMB issue is present.
WD 2TB HMB BSOD fix firmware. Firmware 731130WD or later is the vendor-recommended baseline before evaluating nvmedisk.sys.
2TB SN770M HMB BSOD risk before firmware 731130WD. Update firmware first; nvmedisk.sys can compound instability while the HMB issue is present.
WD 2TB HMB BSOD fix firmware. Firmware 281050WD or later is the vendor-recommended baseline before evaluating nvmedisk.sys.
2TB SN580 HMB BSOD risk before firmware 281050WD. Update firmware first; nvmedisk.sys can compound instability while the HMB issue is present.
WD 2TB HMB BSOD fix firmware. Firmware 291020WD or later is the vendor-recommended baseline before evaluating nvmedisk.sys.
2TB SN5000 HMB BSOD risk before firmware 291020WD. Update firmware first; nvmedisk.sys can compound instability while the HMB issue is present.
WD/SanDisk 2TB HMB BSOD fix firmware. Firmware 731130WD or later is the vendor-recommended baseline before evaluating nvmedisk.sys.
2TB SanDisk Extreme M.2 NVMe HMB BSOD risk before firmware 731130WD. Update firmware first; nvmedisk.sys can compound instability while the HMB issue is present.
Crucial P3 - some reports of paging errors under sustained 4K random write on early firmware. Benchmark with the watchdog active.
Mixed community benchmark result: stable reports exist, but gains are negligible on some P41 systems. Benchmark before/after and keep rollback ready.
Phison E18-based drive. Community reports warn about power-loss corruption risk under nvmedisk.sys write acknowledgement behavior; use UPS/power protection and keep backups current.
Intel 670p / 665p - known to hang under Native NVMe. Keep on stornvme.sys.
Phison-based Rocket models need current firmware plus power-loss protection; community reports warn about RAW partition corruption risk during writes under nvmedisk.sys.
Early Phison E18 firmware - avoid Native NVMe, update drive first.
Community reports warn Phison E18 platforms can expose RAW partition corruption after power loss while writes are in flight under nvmedisk.sys. Use UPS/power protection and keep backups current.
Community reports warn Phison E26 platforms can expose RAW partition corruption after power loss while writes are in flight under nvmedisk.sys. Use UPS/power protection and keep backups current.
Phison-based MP600 models need current firmware plus power-loss protection; community reports warn about RAW partition corruption risk during writes under nvmedisk.sys.
Phison E18-based drive. Community reports warn about RAW partition corruption risk during writes under nvmedisk.sys; use UPS/power protection and keep backups current.

cveAdvisories.description: Kernel RCE in Windows Server 2025 NVMe-oF (NVMe over Fabrics). Does NOT affect local nvmedisk.sys on Windows 11 client - listed here so Server 2025 operators of this tool who also use NVMe-oF patch promptly, and so client users don't panic over the headline.
